DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT PETER ACWORTH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES FROM PLAINTIFF CAMERON ADAMS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. MS. ADAMS IS HEREBY ORDERED TO PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT SIGNED VERIFICATION OF HER PREVIOUSLY SERVED AMENDED RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 137 WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT SUSTAINS DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS 2, 3, AND 4 TO THE DECLARATION OF SANDRA RIBERA SPEED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL AND OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION 1. PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE A FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 137. PLAINTIFF MAINTAINS THAT FOR A VARIETY OF REASONS RELATED TO CONDITIONS AND CIRCUMSTANCES AT THE ADULT FILM SHOOT SHE CANNOT TRUTHFULLY IDENTIFY WHICH OF THE LISTED PARTICIPANTS IS THE INDIVIDUAL SHE CONTENDS INFECTED HER WITH HIV. DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO FORCE PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE AN UNTRUTHFUL ANSWER TO AN INTERROGATORY. SEE, SCHEIDING V. DINWIDDIE CONST. CO. (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 64, 76: "PARTIES MUST STATE THE TRUTH, THE WHOLE TRUTH, AND NOTHING BUT THE TRUTH IN ANSWERING WRITTEN INTERROGATORIES." MOREOVER, DEFENDANT CITES TO NO LEGAL AUTHORITY IN SUPPORT OF HIS ARGUMENT AS TO PLAINTIFF'S BURDEN OF PROOF ON CAUSATION AND THE EVIDENCE DEFENDANT CLAIMS PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE TO MEET HER BURDEN OF PROOF, AND IN ANY CASE THIS ARGUMENT IS NOT SUBJECT TO A MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: MELINDA DERISH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MAXIMILLIAN ALEXANDER CONTRERAS, CSR #13876, TEL: 415-987-5746 (302/JPT)

DEFENDANT PETER ACWORTH MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY.

Appearing counsel for parties all stipulate to the matter being heard by the judge pro tem. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Peter Acworth's Motion to Compel Further Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Cameron Adams is granted in part. Ms. Adams is hereby ordered to provide code-compliant signed verification of her previously served Amended Response to defendant's Special Interrogatory No. 137 within fifteen (15) days of notice of entry of this order. The court sustains defendant's objections 2, 3, and 4 to the Declaration of Sandra Ribera Speed in Support of Plaintiff's Opposition to Defendant's Motion to Compel and overrules defendant's objection 1. Plaintiff is not required to provide a further response to Special Interrogatory No. 137. Plaintiff maintains that for a variety of reasons related to conditions and circumstances at the adult film shoot she cannot truthfully identify which of the listed participants is the individual she contends infected her with HIV. Defendant is not entitled to force plaintiff to provide an untruthful answer to an interrogatory. See, Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 76: "Parties must state the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth in answering written interrogatories." Moreover, defendant cites to no legal authority in support of his argument as to plaintiff's burden of proof on causation and the evidence defendant claims plaintiff must provide to meet her burden of proof, and in any case this argument is not subject to a motion to compel discovery. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the court for signature that complies with CRC 3.1312. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS KINK STUDIOS, LLC, CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC, ARMORY STUDIOS, LLC, PETER ACWORTH AND SARA CORRIGAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Although plaintiff does not expressly allege that she was an independent contractor, nothing in the complaint or that is judicially noticeable establishes that she was an employee. The determination whether a person is an employee or independent contractor is done on a case by case basis involving the consideration of numerous factors. (S.G. Borello & Sons, Inc. v. State Dept. of Industrial Relations (1989) 48 Cal.3d. 341.) The Cal-OSHA Appeals Board Decision does not establish that plaintiff was an employee in this particular case. Nor does plaintiff's workers' compensation claim establish that plaintiff was an employee. The status of the workers' compensation proceeding is unclear and it is premature to determine whether judicial estoppel applies. Moreover, facts alleged in the complaint support exceptions to the exclusivity rule. (Labor Code 3601(a)(1) (injury caused by the willful and unprovoked physical act of aggression of another employee); 3602(b)(1) (willful physical assault) & (2) (fraudulent concealment aggravates injury). The alleged OSHA violations support plaintiff's negligence per se claim. (See Elsner v. Uveges (2004) 34 Cal.4th 915, 928.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
